markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Create roundtrip fixtures for every block/embed/link type plus conflict and missing-target cases. This MT belongs to RichDocumentCore. Build the canonical structured document model backing the editor, Loom, UserManual, and projections.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Rich documents are PostgreSQL/EventLedger/CRDT-backed structured block documents, not Markdown vault authority.
All rich document state must preserve Raw/Derived/Display separation and stable entity IDs.
scope.expected_behavior: RichDocumentFixtures leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: RichDocumentFixtures is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified (commits b506d832 backend core + 935e0482 manual + bf0c62a5 integration tests + dd3056f5 frontend); RichDocumentCore: 6 real-PG integration + 27 unit backend tests + 8 vitest (app suite 349/349). 18 block kinds, RawDerivedDisplay (CX-100), stable KBL- ids, save/load API w/ receipts, projections (negative proof: projection-delete leaves authority byte-identical), embeds (absolute paths DB-CHECK rejected), backlinks (stable KDLNK- relationship_id), history, permissions, crash recovery; migrations 0280-0282; RichDocumentView.tsx wired to real authority with stable selectors for MT-161+ GUI groups. UserManual coverage added (1 group + 10 cmd refs).

findings.title: Fixtures miss the broken/round-trip cases the contract calls out
findings.evidence: knowledge_document_model_tests.rs mixed_document + roundtrip cover paragraph/heading/code/link; no importedRaw round-trip (the case that breaks load, MT-151), no slideshow/album/video embed round-trip through BlockTree+projection, no explicit conflict fixture.
findings.fix: Add fixtures for every block/embed/link kind incl. importedRaw, plus conflict and missing-target cases per the contract.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-160 (RichDocumentFixtures) requires roundtrip fixtures for every block/embed/link type plus conflict and missing-target cases, backed by PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority. The branch delivers real-PostgreSQL fixtures covering all 21 block kinds (byte-identical save/load roundtrip), typed-embed rejection of absolute paths via a real DB CHECK, projections-never-authority, crash recovery (promoted and draft), stale-version Conflict, and broken/missing-target embed repair. The code is real, not placeholder, and aligns with current Master Spec 2.3.13.11.
findings: Every block/embed/link type roundtrip IS proven: src/backend/handshake_core/tests/knowledge_tiptap_roundtrip_fixture_tests.rs:40-111 builds all_custom_nodes_doc covering all 21 BlockKind variants (heading, paragraph w/ all hsLink inline kinds incl video/album/slideshow + mention + tagMention, bulletList, orderedList, codeBlock, monacoCodeBlock, table, taskList, blockquote, image, video, album, slideshow, fileLink, folderLink, projectLink, specLink, wpLink, symbolLink, importedRaw); L455-490 asserts the exact kinds vector AND tree.to_document_json() == loaded.content_json after real-PG create/save/load. This is the every-type roundtrip the MT-160 scope demands.

Conflict case proven on a real rich document against real PostgreSQL: tests/knowledge_documents_tests.rs:294-308 saves v2 then re-saves with stale expected_version=1 and asserts expect_err matches StorageError::Conflict(_). Backed by the save_knowledge_rich_document_version optimistic-concurrency path (storage/knowledge.rs:1794-1795 comment + StorageError::Conflict).

Missing-target case proven in the MT-160 integration file itself: tests/knowledge_document_model_tests.rs:319-363 creates an embed to KMED-missing, marks it broken, asserts it lands in the broken-embed queue, repairs it, and asserts the queue drains (MT-152/153 missing-target repair).

Real Handshake-owned resource touch (sub-rule 2): all fixtures run on real Handshake-managed PostgreSQL via knowledge_pg() isolated schemas and real PostgresDatabase::connect (knowledge_document_model_tests.rs:480-483, 546-549). The absolute-path rejection hits a real DB CHECK chk_knowledge_document_embeds_ref_value_not_path via raw sqlx INSERT (L302-316). No SQLite, no implementer-authored mock substitutes for the durable authority.

Projections-never-authority negative proof present: knowledge_document_model_tests.rs:370-446 registers a wiki projection, deletes it, and asserts the authority RichDocument row is byte-identical before/after, matching Master Spec 2.3.13.11 (02-system-architecture.md:2711 'Deleting or editing a projection MUST NOT mutate authority records').

Crash recovery proven for both promoted (L453-506) and draft write-box (L514-590) state via a fresh PostgresDatabase connection, reconstructing version history + block tree with no data loss and no silent promotion.

No placeholder/deferred-live escapes in any of the three fixture/test files (grep for todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/Unavailable/'deferred to follow'/'pending MT-' returns nothing); SKIP-on-no-PG guards are standard env gates, not stand-ins.

The coder's own adversarial-review-v2 LOW finding (missing importedRaw/album/slideshow/video roundtrip + conflict fixture) is now actually fixed in the branch by the MT-236 fixture file and the documents-tests conflict case; that finding is no longer reachable as a real gap. Aside (non-blocking): the every-type roundtrip lives in a file labeled MT-236 rather than the MT-160 file, and link-level missing-target (unresolved hsLink) is not separately asserted beyond embed missing-target -- neither is a code/behavior defect against the MT scope, which is satisfied across the committed suite.
All three fixture files are git-tracked and the working tree is clean (HEAD c7491525); implementation commits b506d832+935e0482+bf0c62a5+dd3056f5 plus c49afb7f for the all-nodes fixture.
evidence_checked: git show --stat bf0c62a5 / dd3056f5; git log --grep MT-160; git ls-files + status for test files

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
